Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable for Diego Castaneda's relocation record. Tool: airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, then airtable_search_records (field "Name", value "Diego Castaneda") or airtable_list_records on tblRelocations01. Discovers recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin: PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn to Austin, In Progress, Apr 12 to May 2, coordinator Javier Morales. Special Requirements: Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill (~600 lbs, $42K insured), vibration-dampened crating, climate-controlled transport 60-80F, pre-move crating inspection Apr 10-11, 16-day Austin storage (Apr 15 to May 1), dock-level access required, $50K cargo insurance minimum.
OE 2: Search emails for Diego's crating inspection results. The prompt says "the crating inspection already happened and the crew told him the weight came in heavier." Tool: search_emails with query related to "Diego" or "Castaneda" or "crating" or "inspection" (or similar). Discovers email from diego.castaneda@pivotpointlabs.com confirming Heartland crew did the on-site crating April 11, but the crate came out at approximately 680 lbs (not the original 600 lb estimate) due to extra anti-vibration padding.
OE 3: Search emails for SecureStore correspondence about Diego's storage. The prompt says "SecureStore sent me something about the rate." Tool: search_emails with query related to "SecureStore" or "Diego" or "storage" or "Austin" (or similar). Discovers two emails: (1) initial confirmation at $45/day for 16 days ($720) at Congress Ave facility, Unit 14B, climate-controlled with dock access; (2) follow-up rate revision adding $15/day oversize surcharge for remaining 8 days because the CNC mill exceeded standard pallet dimensions, revised total $840.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for PivotPoint invoice to compare against actual vendor costs. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria for PivotPoint (or similar). Discovers INV-2026-0315 with Diego's storage line at $640 (16 days x $40/day). Vendor actual is $840. Invoice-to-vendor gap: $200. Also notes crating at $1,200 and climate freight at $850 on the invoice.
OE 5: Search Airtable for Keiko Tanaka's relocation record. Tool: airtable_search_records (field "Name", value "Keiko Tanaka") or from the earlier list_records. Discovers recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle: Mosaic Robotics, Columbus to Seattle, In Progress, Apr 15-23, coordinator Javier Morales. Special Requirements: Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter (Class 3B CO2), DOT 49 CFR Part 173 hazmat placarding required, Swift must sign laser liability waiver, $800 hazmat handling surcharge, 8-day interim storage at Swift Seattle ($450 flat fee), 1BR near University District with freight elevator or ground floor.
OE 6: Search Linear for the Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance ticket. The prompt says "there was a question about whether the placarding paperwork ever got signed." Tool: linear_list_issues with query related to "Keiko" or "Tanaka" or "DOT" or "placarding" or "hazmat" (or similar). Discovers issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic ("DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser"), state in_progress, placarding certificate listed as MISSING. Also discovers subtask issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert ("Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift"), state_todo.
OE 7: Search emails for Swift's confirmation that placarding was completed. The prompt says "Greg at Swift sent Chloe something about it." Tool: search_emails with query related to "placarding" or "Greg" or "Swift" or "DOT" or "Keiko" (or similar). Discovers Greg Pallone's reply to Chloe confirming placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra at Columbus pickup April 15 under 49 CFR 172.504, signed and countersigned at Swift Seattle, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538. Scanned PDFs to follow within 24 hours.
OE 8: Search emails for Swift's storage billing for Keiko. The prompt says "the flat rate we quoted does not match what Swift is billing." Tool: search_emails with query related to "Keiko" or "storage" or "Swift" or "Seattle" (or similar). Discovers Greg Pallone's email stating actual storage was 9 days (not 8) at $65/day for oversized equipment requiring climate control ($585), plus $120 Class 3B laser climate surcharge, total $705. This contradicts the $450 flat rate in the Airtable record and on the client invoice.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for Mosaic invoice to compare against vendor actuals. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria for Mosaic (or similar). Discovers INV-2026-0411 with Keiko's hazmat surcharge at $800 and interim storage at $450. Storage vendor actual is $705. Invoice-to-vendor gap: $255.
OE 10: Update the Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance Linear ticket with Swift's confirmation. The prompt implies fixing the compliance status. Tool: linear_update_issue on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic with updated state and description noting Greg Pallone confirmed placarding completion. Also update subtask issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert.
OE 11: Search emails for the BrightLoop wrong-city apartment. The prompt says "the housing vendor apparently sourced an apartment in the wrong city for one of my BrightLoop employees." Tool: search_emails with query related to "Jordan" or "Whitfield" or "BrightLoop" or "apartment" (or similar). Discovers Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest confirmed a furnished 1BR at 215 Hanover St, North End, Boston for Jordan Whitfield, but Jordan's Airtable record shows Minneapolis to Denver.
OE 12: Search Slack for Blessing's flag about the wrong-city apartment. Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on C006. Discovers Blessing's message noting Jordan Whitfield's UrbanNest confirmation says Boston but Airtable says Denver, suggesting Carmen mixed him up with another BrightLoop employee.
OE 13: Cross-check Jordan Whitfield's Airtable record. Tool: airtable_search_records for Jordan Whitfield. Confirms destination is Denver, CO - not Boston. Agent identifies the city mismatch.
OE 14: Search emails for the Vectral housing situation. The prompt says "a Vectral employee stuck in a hotel in Denver because her original apartment fell through." Tool: search_emails with query related to "Vectral" or "Denver" or "apartment" or "housing" (or similar). Discovers Carmen Reyes's URGENT email about Priya Suresh: RiNo landlord reversed pet approval for Atlas (85-lb German Shepherd, 50-lb breed-specific insurance limit). Alternative: Highlands unit at $2,450/month ($200 more), needs EOD April 17 confirmation. Also discovers Priya Suresh's email about being in a hotel, boxes in RiNo lobby with April 19 deadline, asking who covers the $200/month difference.
OE 15: Search emails for the Heartland charge on another move. The prompt says "check if there is anything from Heartland about a charge on one of my other moves that does not look right." Tool: search_emails with query related to "Heartland" or "Ravi" or "Sundaram" or "billing" or "reconciliation" (or similar). Discovers Jake Loomis at Heartland claiming a $1,600 charge for Ravi Sundaram (Tideway, Tampa to Denver, Apr 14-16) with a signed pickup confirmation, asking Javier to confirm.
OE 16: Cross-check Ravi Sundaram's Airtable record. Tool: airtable_search_records for Ravi Sundaram. Discovers the record says "Standard+ package. Data analyst." with no moving vendor specified. Agent must determine whether Heartland's claim is valid or a phantom charge.
OE 17: Identify account managers for affected clients. Tool: crm_search_companies for "Vectral" and "BrightLoop" and "Mosaic" and "PivotPoint" (or similar), then crm_search_contacts or contacts_search_contacts. Discovers Mina Hashimoto manages Vectral (Priya) and BrightLoop (Jordan), Catalina Dubois manages PivotPoint (Diego), Emeka Diallo manages Mosaic (Keiko).
OE 18: Update Airtable relocation records with corrected information. Tool: airtable_update_records on tblRelocations01. Updates: Diego Castaneda (crate weight 680 lbs, storage revised to $840, SecureStore oversize surcharge), Keiko Tanaka (DOT placarding confirmed by Swift, storage revised from $450 to $705, 9 days not 8), Jordan Whitfield (flag wrong-city apartment - Boston confirmation error, needs Denver apartment), Priya Suresh (RiNo failed, Highlands alternative $2,450/month, employee in hotel, boxes deadline Apr 19), Ravi Sundaram (note Heartland $1,600 claim pending verification).
OE 19: Create Linear issues to track outstanding items. The prompt says to create tickets for follow-up. Tool: linear_create_issue. Items: Diego SecureStore cost discrepancy ($640 invoiced vs $840 actual), Jordan Whitfield wrong-city apartment correction with UrbanNest, Priya Suresh housing resolution (Highlands confirmation, box relocation), Ravi Sundaram Heartland billing verification.
OE 20: Notify Chloe Vance about client-facing issues so she can loop in account managers. The prompt says "if any of this is going to be a problem for the account managers, let Chloe know so she can loop them in." Tool: send_email (or reply_to_email) to chloe.vance@moveops.com. Content: Priya Suresh housing crisis (Vectral - Mina's account), Jordan Whitfield wrong-city apartment (BrightLoop - Mina's account), Keiko Tanaka storage cost change (Mosaic - Emeka's account).
OE 21: Flag invoice discrepancies to Chloe and Hana. The prompt says "If Chloe or Hana need to know about cost differences between what we invoiced and what vendors are actually charging, flag that for them." Tool: send_email (or reply_to_email) to chloe.vance@moveops.com and/or hana.kim@moveops.com. Content: Diego storage $640 invoiced vs $840 actual, Keiko storage $450 invoiced vs $705 actual, Ravi Sundaram potential $1,600 Heartland phantom charge.
OE 22: Post status update in #operations channel. The prompt says "post a status update in the ops channel." Tool: conversations_add_message to channel C006 (#operations). Content: Diego CNC mill coordination status (crating done at 680 lbs, storage secured but rate revised), Keiko laser status (DOT placarding confirmed, storage cost mismatch, compliance ticket updated), Jordan apartment city error, Priya housing crisis, Ravi Heartland billing question.
OE 23: Contact UrbanNest to correct Jordan Whitfield's apartment. The prompt implies fixing vendor issues. Tool: send_email (or reply_to_email) to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com explaining the Boston/Denver mix-up and requesting a Denver apartment search for Jordan with his April 22 move-in date.
OE 24: Address Priya Suresh's housing situation with UrbanNest. Tool: send_email (or reply_to_email) to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com confirming the Highlands apartment or requesting updated options, and asking about the RiNo deposit refund timeline and the box situation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records).
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓—— 100%
